entry.title: Disable row select in Fiori element List report app with Grid Table and Odata V4 based on condition.
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I have a requirement where I need disable the row select based on backed conditions in Grid table Fiori element List report.

I am able to achieve it with Responsive table where the row is greyed out but its making the navigation to object page disable as well and due to which other column values are unknown.

I need to change the table to Grid table but in V4, I am not getting table object instance in OnAfterRendering event so that I use UI table events.

Kindly please help as this requirement is critical for business and I am struggling because of it.

Help and suggestions are much appreciated.

Thanks,

Best Regards,

Raina

entry.title: SAC’s Time Hierarchy limitation with SuccessFactors Live OData Connection
entry.content:
Summary

We need to configure a month-, quarter-, and year-level headcount and attrition time-series analyses over a history in SAC, based on the SuccessFactors fields Date of Joining and Last Working Date. However, under a live OData connection from SuccessFactors, SAC treats those fields as plain dimensions—because OData maps ABAP’s date types to Edm.DateTime and SAC’s live connector doesn’t infer the SAC “Date” semantic type—which prevents SAC’s native time hierarchies (Year → Quarter → Month → Week → Day) from being available. The following post outlines the business context, the precise problem face : 

1. Business Scenario

To build time-series analyses for headcount and attrition metrics leveraging live data from SAP SuccessFactors. The end users require:

  • Headcount snapshots of total active employees at each period end, and

  • Attrition rates calculated as leavers during the period divided by average headcount,
    broken down by calendar year, quarter, month, and week, with drill-down capability and filters by department, region, job family, and other organizational dimensions. 

This must operate entirely over the existing live OData connection—we cannot import or replicate data to any other platform (BW, HANA, Datasphere or WFA). 

2. Problem Statement

Under SAC’s live OData connection to SuccessFactors, fields such as Date of Joining and Last Working Date arrive as Edm.DateTime properties in the OData service and are ingested by SAC as generic dimensions without any date semantics. As a result:

  • SAC’s predefined calendar hierarchies (Year → Quarter → Month → Week → Day) are not generated for these dimensions, because SAC only auto-creates them on dimensions explicitly tagged with the SAC Date semantic type in import models. 

  • Model-level configuration is read-only in a live model, so you cannot reassign semantic types or define hierarchies for these fields within SAC. 

  • Native drill-down functionality in charts—users clicking from Year down to Month or Week—is unavailable for these date attributes, blocking standard time-series analytics. 

I’m looking for suggestions or ideas on how to tackle a specific requirement using a live OData-only SAC model. If anyone knows of any workarounds, lesser-known features, or upcoming updates that might help in this scenario, I’d really appreciate it. Thanks!

entry.title: field link is greyed out in list report
entry.content:
Hi experts,

hope you can help us to solve this problem:

Created a Fiori Element app with an oData V4. One field in the listreport should be a link. The problem is that the link is greyed out. 
In the consumption view

'http://www.sap.com' as full_url

In the metadata

@ui: {lineItem: [ { position: 70, importance: #HIGH, label: 'Link' , type: #WITH_URL, targetElement: 'full_url'} ]}

The app preview in eclipse, preview in BAS and in the FLP, link is greyed out and can't be clicked. There is no navigation to an object page. 

our UI5 version is '1.120.19' .

Why is the link greyed out? and how to make it clickable?

Thanks in advanced,

Lee

entry.title: SAP OData WebService DataModel for CREATE-Operation - Am i doing this the intended way?
entry.content:
I am currently working on a project to upgrade existing SAP Function Models from external RFC to OData Rest-Services.

Certain things are set:

  • it has to be OData and REST
  • the interface has to be as close to the original as possible

The given Function Model has the followong architecture:

  • IMPORT
    • iv_testrun type BOOL
  • EXPORT
    • es_return like BAPIRET2
  • TABLES
    • IT_TIMETICKETS
    • ET_DETAIL_RETURN

The function model works relativly easy:

  • IMPORT is self-explanatory
  • EXPORT is given if something goes wrong which kills the processing
  • TABLES
    • the timetickets which shall be created to an existing order
    • the detailed return gives a message for each given ticket if the creation was succesfull or not and some details what went wrong

Knowing my way around the SEGW and the DPC, DPC_EXT, MPC, MPC_EXT -Classes i designed a webservice with a deep_entity setup.

  1. i created a KEY-Entity with a literal Key called ConfCreate to later connect the importing Values to.
  2. i created the VALUE-Entity which has basically all the inputs of IT_TIMETICKETS. This i connecte via an association with the KEY-Entity settings set on 1 to M.
  3. To get the detailed results i went in my opinion a bit sketchi... i created a third entity called RETURN. this on i also connected to the KEY-Entity with an association.

so overall the segw looks something like this:

and the associations look like this:

lastly i redefined the CREATE_DEEP_ENTITY Method of the DPC_EXT and voilà, it works...
...but its kind of ugly and it feels "done in the wrong way"

so this is the request:

{
  "Testrun": "",
  "Values": {
    "results": [
      {
        "ConfNo": "1",
        "Orderid": "",
        "Operation": "",
        "SubOper": "",
        "CapaCategory": ""
      },
      {
        "ConfNo": "2",
        "Orderid": "",
        "Operation": "",
        "SubOper": "",
        "CapaCategory": ""
      }
    ]
  },
  "Returns": []
}

ConfCreate is, as already explained, the "link" to the Value-Entries.
Testrun defines if it´s a testrun or not. Since both have to be adressable i defined them as KEYS.
And in Values, we have the timeticket-values which we can give noumerous at once into the service.

... And then there is results ... 
this is left blank because it is needed to be automatically in the tech_clauses, which is a string_table defining what goes in AND comes out.

If there should be a general error we get a 40X or 50X http status and if there is a business wrongdoing we get the results filled as intended

this is an example response:

[...]
    "Returns": {
      "results": [
        {
          "__metadata": {
            "id": "https://.../sap/opu/odata/sap/Z...CONF_CREATE_SRV_01/ReturnSet(ConfCreate='',Testrun='')",
            "uri": "https://.../sap/opu/odata/sap/Z...CONF_CREATE_SRV_01/ReturnSet(ConfCreate='',Testrun='')",
            "type": "Z...CONF_CREATE_SRV_01.Return"
          },
          "ConfCreate": "",
          "Testrun": "",
          "Type": "E",
          "MessageId": "RU",
          "MessageNumber": "020",
          "Message": "Rückmeldenr. 1 nicht vorhanden (Bitte Eingabe prüfen)",
          "LogNumber": "",
          "LogMsgNo": "000000",
          "MessageV1": "1",
          "MessageV2": "",
          "MessageV3": "",
          "MessageV4": "",
          "Parameter": "",
          "Row": 1,
          "Field": "",
          "System": "A05_010",
          "FlgLocked": false,
          "ConfNo": "0000000000",
          "ConfCnt": "00000000"
        },
        {
          "__metadata": {
            "id": "https://.../sap/opu/odata/sap/Z...CONF_CREATE_SRV_01/ReturnSet(ConfCreate='',Testrun='')",
            "uri": "https://.../sap/opu/odata/sap/Z...CONF_CREATE_SRV_01/ReturnSet(ConfCreate='',Testrun='')",
            "type": "Z...CONF_CREATE_SRV_01.Return"
          },
          "ConfCreate": "",
          "Testrun": "",
          "Type": "E",
          "MessageId": "RU",
          "MessageNumber": "020",
          "Message": "Rückmeldenr. 2 nicht vorhanden (Bitte Eingabe prüfen)",
          "LogNumber": "",
          "LogMsgNo": "000000",
          "MessageV1": "2",
          "MessageV2": "",
          "MessageV3": "",
          "MessageV4": "",
          "Parameter": "",
          "Row": 2,
          "Field": "",
          "System": "A05_010",
          "FlgLocked": false,
          "ConfNo": "0000000000",
          "ConfCnt": "00000000"
        }
      ]
    }
  }
}

so now we come to my question:

Is this really the intended way of solving sth like this?

Is there a more fitting configuration and setup hidden inside the SEGW i do not know yet?

So please, if someone is experienced with mass-creates and explicit returns for each individual create please let me know.

 

Kind Regards
Mirco

entry.title: Unable to load $metadata within web dev tools - "Browser not supporting Javascript" error
entry.content:
Hi All, 

Managed to connect odata service through simpleproxy into ui5 application, but when viewing the $metadata within network tab i get a "Note: Since your browser does notsupport JavaScript, you must press the Continuebutton once to" issue,

the continue button does nothing.

the odata service is behind a SSO authentication, could this be the cause of this issue?

help would be much appreciated.

entry.title: Create a header with items in a single OData request from a Fiori app using XML annotations
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I would like to create a Fiori application for purchase contracts with BAPI. I have an OData service with associations and a Fiori application with XML annotations. It works—I can see the header and the associated items on the object page in BAS. I am able to create new items, but only the header and items separately. I would like to create them using a single OData request, so I redefined get_expanded_entityset and create_deep_entity.

It works using the GW Client.

However, I cannot find any information on how to use this functionality in Fiori with XML annotations. Is it possible? How can I create a header with items in a single request?

entry.title: Reporting on completed workflows
entry.content:
Hi,
We have set up Custom MDF's where there are the following participants of a workflow:
Initiator: The manager initiates a workflow when adding the MDF to his employee.
Approver: This goes to a seperate approver
Employee: The employee is the final step. He/she checks the workflow and approves if all is OK.

That all works as expected of course, but when these workflows are completed, we have a need of picking them up and archiving them. Normally I would just read the data of the MDF from Odata API on custom MDF object of user. But I also need to fetch information about the approvers in the workflow, and at what date they signed/approved the workflow.

I was hoping to be able to do this with the latest release of 1H 2025, but cant seem to figure out how to fetch the participants, and date of approvals.

With only one initiator and one final approver, I can store the user and date in the MDF itself, as the creation of the MDF is triggering a save (so I can populate hidden fields) and the final accept of the workflow is also triggering a save (so I can populate final details). But with an approver in the middle, not changing anything (only approving or sending back), there is no save action, so no way to pick up the user and date of this "middle" approver. Hence the need to access this from API after workflow is completed.

Any tips on this would be great!

See image for reference. This is what I can see when browsing completed workflows from the "Workflow Requests Page". I want to fetch Initiator, Approver and Employee (final approver) as shown here, with userID/name and date of approval.

entry.title: Extend/Copy CDS view for fiori app
entry.content:
Hi All,

There is a standard Fiori app for compare supplier quotation which displays the quotations which are created via Fiori app. This Fiori app is built based on CDS view/OData. My requirement is exactly same but to fetch quotations which are created in GUI. The CDS view(I_RequestForQuotation) where condition has hardcoding to bring the quotations of particular document category. I cannot edit where condition since it is standard. 

Can anyone suggest me how can i achieve this functionality?

Is copying the whole list of objects (Fiori app/CDS views/OData/UI5) to custom is the only way or any other possible simple solutions here?

I can provide more information on the requirement if needed.

Thanks,

Sai.

entry.title: post array using ODataV4 / V2
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

 

I have this JSON Post in Odata 

{
"brand" : "ShopRite",
"fundingType" : 10,
"offerCode" : "25041101",
"gs1CoPrefix" : "",
"familyCode" : "0",
"offerSeries" : "1",
"manufacturerCodes" : [ "text", "text2" ],
"taxesReclaimable" : false,
"isTender" : false
}

manufacturerCodes is the array expected by the client.

How can we configure and implement a system to handle arrays in SEGW for OData? I know there are complex types or Collection types in V4, but those require a property (the sub-object has a field label like)  

"manufacturerCodes" : [  code: "text",

                                        code "text2" ]

or 

"manufacturerCodes" : [ { code: "text", }

                                       { code: "text2"  }

                                  ],

Any idea? Is there any standard approach to post a field with an array to SAP OData?

If we post, I'm having this error: "error while parsing an XML stream". Already tried using a collection or complex type.

 

Please
Thanks a lot for your time to help

entry.title: Create custom entity with compositions exposed as OData V4 API
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

I have a question which I am not able to solve by myself ^^

My requirement basically is to create a new custom API in SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud that allows me, for a given {warehouse + batch-managed product + quantity}, to find the list of all batch numbers, that can be consumed to address that batch-managed product's quantity based on some rules.

I gave myself as objective to create this structure as output of my new custom API, with a header structure (giving the status of the batch determination logic) and an item list (giving the list of all the batch numbers that should be used for the consumption, with the quantity to be consumed in each batch):

I started by created 2 custom CDS entities: one for the header structure and another one for the item structure.

I added this annotation to both these 2 custom CDS entities "@ObjectModel.query.implementedBy:'ABAP:ZCL_TEST_BATCH_NBR_DET1'" where "ZCL_TEST_BATCH_NBR_DET1'" is a custom class referencing interface "IF_RAP_QUERY_PROVIDER" In order to be able to trigger its method "SELECT" when someone tries to retrieve data from either one of my 2 custom CDS entities. 

In order to have a parent-child relationship between my 2 custom CDS entities, I added a composition statement in the header custom entity CDS view and an association to parent statement in the line-items child custom entity CDS view.

Custom Entity for the Header parent CDS view:

Custom Entity for the Line-Item Child CDS view:

Then I implemented code for the SELECT statement of my custom class ZCL_TEST_BATCH_NBR_DET1.

Once done, I created a new service definition exposing both my 2 custom cds entities:

Then I exposed my service definition into an OData V4 API service binding, followed with the normal communication scenario, comm arrangement configuration..

My problem is that, once all this is done, when I try to execute my API from POSTMAN, I am not able to get the header AND the item data AT THE SAME TIME.

I am able for instance to get data for the header custom entity CDS view with the correct structure I defined: 

I am also able to get data for the line_item custom entity CDS view, for the different batch numbers to use for the consumption as per the structure I designed:

But, How can I merge these two results of these 2 entities in the same response?

I want my custom API response to be similar to the following one: 

How can I accomplish this please?

I thought by creating a composition relationship between these 2 custom entities, that the SELECT method will fetch data for both header and Item custom entity CDS views and that the result will be merged in the same response, but it is not.

Also, when I run a GET OData call to the header entity, "SELECT" method of class "ZCL_TEST_BATCH_NBR_DET0" is called only once, and "io_request->get_entity_id( )" gives "ZC_TEST_BATCH_DETERMIN_HD" as entity. This means, that my logic is being executed ONLY on the header custom CDS view, which is not enough to fulfill my requirement.

Then I tried to add "$expand" to the OData URL to execute, by adding "?$expand=_BatchDetItemDetails" to my URL:

but I am getting HTTP Bad Request error in POSTMAN : "

"The expand option's expand path contains an invalid property '_BatchDetItemDetails'",

Can someone help me on this please? I feel like my custom API is not designed to "accept" any "expand" attribute. 

Or if you have another idea on how to achieve this, without having to use the "?$expand" statement?

PS: the metadata of this custom API is attached to the present message

Thanks experts

entry.title: Issue with Finding Entities in SAP Standard API for Equipment MarkForDeletion Functionality
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

I am currently working on utilizing the SAP Standard API for equipment management, specifically to implement the "MarkForDeletion" functionality. I referred to the API details on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, where I found the following endpoints related to equipment deletion:

  • POST /ResetEQUIPFromInactive
  • POST /SetEQUIPToInactive
  • POST /SetEQUIPToMarkedForDeletion

I have attached a snapshot from the Business Accelerator Hub showing these endpoints for reference (Snapshot 1).

However, when I checked the available entity sets in the SAP Gateway Client using transaction code /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE for the service /sap/opu/odata/sap/API_EQUIPMENT/, I could not find the corresponding entities or operations for the above endpoints. The entity sets I found are limited to:

  • EQUIPClassCharacteristicValue
  • EQUIPMENT
  • EQUIPMENTClass
  • EQUIPMENTClassCharacteristic
  • EQUIPMENTLONGTEXT
  • EQUIPMENTPARTNER
  • EQUIPMENTWARRANTY

I have attached a snapshot from the SAP Gateway Client showing the available entity sets (Snapshot 2).

My questions are:

  1. Where can I find the detailed implementation or metadata for the SetEQUIPToMarkedForDeletion functionality in the SAP system?
  2. Are these endpoints (ResetEQUIPFromInactive, SetEQUIPToInactive, SetEQUIPToMarkedForDeletion) part of a different service or require additional configuration to be visible in the Gateway Client?
  3. How can I proceed with using the "MarkForDeletion" functionality for equipment via the API?

We are keen on using this functionality for our project, so any guidance on how to locate or enable these operations would be greatly appreciated.

Looking forward to your insights!

Attachments:

  • Snapshot 1: Business Accelerator Hub API Endpoints
  • Snapshot 2: SAP Gateway Client Entity Sets

Thank you,

entry.title: OData response time increased significantly after model changes and reduces after 8-9 hours
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am facing a strange issue.

I have built an unmanaged RAP application with 8 entities in SAP on-prem system where all of them are related to each other via associations and compositions. Each entity on an average are having around 25-30 fields. I have created cds, behavior, metadata extensions, service definition and OData V2 UI service binding.

All works good, but the issue is, whenever I change any thing in my model like field logic, or add additional field etc., OData response slows down significantly.

It takes around 35 - 40 seconds to load metadata sometimes it takes even minutes. It again fastens up after 8 - 9 hours and loads in 600 - 800ms.

I tried doing everything including

invalidating global cache

invalidating client cache

invalidating metadata cache

I even tried running report /UI5/UPD_ODATA_METADATA_CACHE

Nothing helps

I would be happy to know what is the potential cause for this and how to resolve

 

metadata loads in 670ms without any modifications.

 

Same service taking 35 seconds after field modification

entry.title: How to add custom fields in Long Term Supplier Declaration Inbound Fiori app in SAP GTS
entry.content:
Hi ,

In SAP GTS , there are no CFL to add custom fields to standard apps . Can you please guide step by step how to add custom fields like producer code and net cost to Long Term Supplier Declaration Inbound Standard Fiori APP .

Also if standard fiori app needs to be copied to custom fiori app , what are the ABAP required changes to be done for custom fiori app.

Detailed Explanation with Example would be appreciated .

Regards,

SN

SAP Global Trade Services  @SAP  SAP Fiori OData

entry.title: Extend CDS View , Redefine ODATA and Enhance BOBF Class to add custom fields in Standard Fiori apps.
entry.content:
How to Extend CDS View with Metadata Extension , Redefine ODATA service and Enhance BOBF class to add custom fields to standard Fiori App in SAP GTS.

Explain with step by step process using any example.

 

SAP Global Trade Services  OData  ABAP Development 

@SAP @sapcommunity @GTS @ABAP

entry.title: How to send multiple default values for a smart filter property from SAP ABAP OData?
entry.content:
Hi,

I am trying to send multiple default values for a smart filter property maintained in UI from SAP ABAP OData service DEFINE method. By using set_default_value I could send only one default value.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: How to filter the list when the column is 1:n relation
entry.content:
Hello,

I am trying to create a webapp using SAP Business Application Studio in BTP. 

The app is connected to S4 HANA Cloud system using oData v2 API

I have view which is a list of products. I want to add a search box to filter the result by Production Description column. Production and Product Description has 1:n relation.

I am using the below source codes. But it doesn't work. 

I get the error in the console - Error during request to remote service: Left hand expression of memberaccess operator has wrong cardinality (to many not allowed)

Can someone please help me?

 

                var aTableSearchState =[];
                var sQuery = oEvent.getParameter("query");

                if (sQuery && sQuery.length > 0) {
                    var ProductTypeFilter = new Filter("ProductType", FilterOperator.Contains, sQuery);
                    var MaterialFilter = new Filter("Material", FilterOperator.Contains, sQuery);
                    var LanguageFilter = new Filter("to_Description/Language", FilterOperator.EQ, 'EN');
                    var DescriptionFilter = new Filter("to_Description/Description", FilterOperator.Contains, sQuery);
                    var QueryFilter1 = new Filter({ filters:[LanguageFilter, DescriptionFilter], and: true});
                    //var QueryFilter = new Filter({ filters:[ProductTypeFilter, MaterialFilter], and: false});
                    var QueryFilter = new Filter({ filters:[ProductTypeFilter, MaterialFilter, QueryFilter1], and: false});
                    aTableSearchState.push(QueryFilter);
                }
                this._applySearch(aTableSearchState);
 
         
_applySearch: function(aTableSearchState) {
          
           var oTable = this.byId("table"),
           oViewModel = this.getModel("worklistView");
            oTable.getBinding("items").filter(aTableSearchState, "Application");
            // changes the noDataText of the list in case there are no filter results
            if (aTableSearchState.length !== 0) {
                oViewModel.setProperty("/tableNoDataText", this.getResourceBundle().getText("worklistNoDataWithSearchText"));
            }

        }

entry.title: Need Help Updating Non-Primitive Properties in OData V4
entry.content:
Hi all,

I've recently started working with OData V4 in SAPUI5, and I'm currently facing an issue when trying to update non-primitive data types such as arrays or objects. From what I’ve read in various blog posts and documentation, OData V4 only supports updating primitive types (e.g., String, Boolean, Number) directly on the entity level via the binding Context.

Here’s a fragment of the code I’m working with:

let oModel = this.getView().getModel();
let sPath = "/ReportsExposed(" + this._oSelectedReportContext.getObject().ID + ")";
let oContextBinding = oModel.bindContext(sPath, null, {
    $$updateGroupId: "SecurityFilterChanges"
});

await oContextBinding.requestObject().then((oData) => {
    oData.securityFilters = [
        {
            filter_ID: "abc-123"
        },
        {
            filter_ID: "def-456"
        }
    ];
});

// Submit batch
oModel.submitBatch("SecurityFilterChanges");

However, the security Filters association (which is an array of related entities) is not being included in the $batch payload, and the backend is not receiving the update.

This is the schema that i am currently working on.

I’m wondering:

  • How can we properly update or create nested (composed or associated) entities using OData V4?

  • Is there a specific pattern or method to handle such cases in the V4 model?

Any guidance or examples would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: SF Onboarding → Celonis via API – only few tasks visible
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

We're currently connecting SuccessFactors Onboarding to Celonis via API, but only get:

  • 2 tasks from “Data Collection”
  • 1 out of 6 “New Hire Tasks”

These show up in Celonis, but only partially – e.g., titles, comments, and content are missing. Other tasks (visible in SF under “Manage Data” with Process ID) don’t come through the API at all.

Question:
Has anyone managed to pull more tasks or fields? Any workarounds (OData, Integration Center, Reports etc.)?

Thanks for sharing your experience!

Best Regards,

Svetlana

SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding API OData

entry.title: Error when try to create an Service Binding via ADT in Eclipse Editor
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I'm trying to create a Service Binding (OData V4 - UI type) via ADT in Eclipse Editor connected to a BTP Trial ABAP Environment, but got the below error message. Anyone could guide on how I should proceed to fix this issue?

 
Thanks in advance!
 
Douglas Marques
 
eclipse.buildId=4.32.0.20240606-1231
java.version=22.0.2
java.vendor=Eclipse Adoptium
BootLoader constants: OS=win32, ARCH=x86_64, WS=win32, NL=en_US
Framework arguments:  -product org.eclipse.epp.package.jee.product
Command-line arguments:  -os win32 -ws win32 -arch x86_64 -product org.eclipse.epp.package.jee.product
 
org.eclipse.e4.ui.workbench
Error
Thu Jun 05 14:38:12 BRT 2025
Cannot invoke "com.sap.adt.util.ui.databinding.DatabindingSectionComposite$DatabindingSectionPart.getSection()" because "this.bindingTypeInfoSectionPart" is null
 
java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "com.sap.adt.util.ui.databinding.DatabindingSectionComposite$DatabindingSectionPart.getSection()" because "this.bindingTypeInfoSectionPart" is null
at com.sap.adt.cds.servicebinding.ui.internal.editors.ServiceBindingPage.createFormBodyContent(ServiceBindingPage.java:79)
at com.sap.adt.cds.servicebinding.ui.internal.editors.ServiceBindingPage.createFormContent(ServiceBindingPage.java:51)
at org.eclipse.ui.forms.editor.FormPage.lambda$0(FormPage.java:166)
at org.eclipse.swt.custom.BusyIndicator.showWhile(BusyIndicator.java:67)
at org.eclipse.ui.forms.editor.FormPage.createPartControl(FormPage.java:166)
at com.sap.adt.tools.core.ui.editors.AdtFormPage.createPartControl(AdtFormPage.java:86)
at com.sap.adt.blues.core.ui.editor.formeditor.BlueAbstractPage.createPartControl(BlueAbstractPage.java:101)
at com.sap.adt.cds.servicebinding.ui.internal.editors.ServiceBindingPage.createPartControl(ServiceBindingPage.java:86)
at org.eclipse.ui.forms.editor.FormEditor.pageChange(FormEditor.java:480)
at com.sap.adt.tools.core.ui.editors.AdtFormEditor.pageChange(AdtFormEditor.java:1532)
at org.eclipse.ui.part.MultiPageEditorPart.setActivePage(MultiPageEditorPart.java:1029)
at org.eclipse.ui.forms.editor.FormEditor.setActivePage(FormEditor.java:612)
at com.sap.adt.tools.core.ui.editors.AdtFormEditor.setActivePage(AdtFormEditor.java:164)

entry.title: Fiori Custom Fields and Logic app error
entry.content:
Dear experts,

we habe a problem with opening the Custom Fields and Logic App on SAP Fiori Launchpad. Everytime you open the app we got the error message that the system is on write protect mode. We looked in the transaction SCC4 and SE06 to look up if the system is proteceted against changes. But the system (specially) the client is not protected against changes therefore we searched the internet for similar errors and we added the fiori katalog into the roles of the user, but also without any success. 

Could anyone of you help us in this matter, we would be very appreciated. 

Best regards,

Adam
